a: Compares two masks.
A type to validate existing AIVU files to ensure that they meet the minimum requirements for AIV.
Prepare and package immersive video content for delivery.
A command type for color fading during immersive media playback.
A value that represents the direction of the fade command.
A value that represents fading in of the video frames from a requested color.
A value that represents fading out of the video frames to a requested color.
A command type for opacity fading environment backdrops during immersive media playback.
A value that represents the direction of the fade environment command.
A value that represents fading in of the environment backdrops.
A value that represents fading out of the environment backdrops.
Read and write essential Apple Immersive Video metadata.
A structure that holds the required information for an immersive media camera to process and render video frames.
A value that represents the type of immersive camera.
A value that represents a camera rig made up of two cameras.
A structure that represents immersive media camera calibration data.
A value that represents the calibration type used to generate camera calibration geometry.
A value that represents a calibration type that uses coefficients to present each camera lens.
A value that represents a calibration type that uses a calibration mesh containing the calibration for each lens represented as a mesh inside a USDZ file.
A type that holds the position information representing the origin from which to render the calibration in 3D space relative to the person’s eye.
A type that holds the matrices used for video frame texture mapping on the camera lens geometry.
This type holds the ILPD lens configuration parameters to generate a camera calibration type instance.
A value that represents a camera lens eye.
A value that represents the left eye.
A value that represents the right eye.
A value that represents a camera lens projection type.
A value that represents an equidistant lens projection type.
A value that represents an equirectangular lens projection type.
A structure that holds the camera mask type information and its relevant mask name.
A value that defines a control points based dynamically generated mask.
A value that defines an image based mask.
Calibration mesh geometry based on USDZ data.
A view model that holds all the resources needed to render an immersive camera view.
A type that holds the information required to dynamically generate an immersive media mask at load time.
A value that represents the interpolation method used while processing the control points.
A value that represents an edge treatment type for the immersive media mask.
A value that determines how the left mask relates to the right, or vice versa.
An object that holds all the information needed to load immersive media masks from image data or from a file.
An object that represents the messaging protocol a remote preview sender and receiver use to communicate.
An observable object that helps applications handle receiving commands and data sent from an immersive media remote preview sender object.
A value that represents the status of the immersive media remote preview receiver object.
An indication that the receiver is in failed state.
An indication that the receiver has already started.
An indication that the receiver is in starting state.
An indication that the receiver is in stopped state.
An observable object that helps an app send the required data to all connected receiver applications to help facilitate the complete preview of the immersive media playback.
An object that renders an immersive video frame into a texture and exposes the command buffer for presentation.
An enumeration that represents the eye of the user.

A type that represents an immersive video frame. An immersive video frame contains: - layout (SideBySide, OverUnder, Separate, Mono) - presentationTime: frame presentation time - pixelBuffers: an array with one or more images representing the frame.
A value that specifies the layout of left and right eyes within an immersive video frame.
A value that represents mono video layout.
A value that represents over-under video layout.
A value that represents stereo video but separated.
A value that represents side by side video layout.
A video mask to use during video rendering to smooth the edges of the mesh.
A value that represents the layout of the video mask.
A value that represents mono mask layout.
A value that represents over-under mask layout.
A value that represents stereo mask, but separated.
A value that represents side by side mask layout.
An object that helps convert the original wide field of view video asset to parametric immersive asset.
An error that occurs during the conversion process of the parametric immersive asset.
A type of an error that may occur when converting the original wide field of view video asset to a parametric immersive asset.
Indicates that the asset was previously converted to parametric immersive format.
Indicates that the asset can’t be converted because it’s missing the required metadata.
Indicates that the asset can’t be converted due to a currently unsupported camera model or capture mode.
A set of properties that define the interface for a presentation command.
A value that represents a command that adds fade-in and fade-out effects during scene transitions.
A value that represents a command that adds fade-in and fade-out effects to backdrop transitions.
A value that represents a command that specifies the camera ID for a specific frame during playback.
A value that represents a command that mirrors a whole frame vertically for the duration of the command.
A value that represents a command that mirrors a whole frame horizontally for the duration of the command.
A structure that represents dynamic metadata used during playback or when outputting the metadata track for an immersive video file.
An object that provides the functionality required to understand and process immersive presentation commands.

t: Processing Apple Immersive Video with foveation
a: Reduce a video’s data rate while maintaining high acuity in the center of the imagery by applying foveation to immersive video content.
